title: Replace hand-made tyre-fade heuristics with causal latent degradation state features
summary: The strongest current code already uses rolling deltas and tyre-life ratios, but recent F1 research shows a state-space degradation model with pit-reset structure outperforms a simpler AR baseline and yields interpretable latent pace-loss states.
rationale: The strongest current code already uses rolling deltas and tyre-life ratios, but recent F1 research shows a state-space degradation model with pit-reset structure outperforms a simpler AR baseline and yields interpretable latent pace-loss states. That suggests the remaining gain is in denoising stint dynamics, not adding more raw lag columns.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race-stint, run a simple causal state filter or exponentially-updated local linear model on lap time after adjusting for race progress/field pace. Emit latent degradation level, latent slope, innovation shock, and uncertainty; reset at pit/stint boundaries.
expected_effect: Better separation of true tyre collapse from noisy laps, traffic, or one-off driver mistakes.
risk: Misspecified filters can overfit clean races and break on wet laps or atypical pace-management regimes.
